frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: Honestly I have no issue with women being ambitious, but once kids come into the picture someone has to prioritise them, and naturally that ends up being the mother more than the father. Corporate jobs can wait a bit, bachpan nahi wait karta, and kids need their mom for the day-to-day things.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes childcare as primarily the mother's responsibility and frames career sacrifice as something women should make once they have children. It encodes the surface manifestations of expecting the mother to prioritize childcare and suggesting a woman's career ambition should come second.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 28. Single. A software engineer in Pune. You are in a Reddit thread on r/India where someone posted about a man who has to pay 80000 rupees per month in alimony to his ex wife who has a job. You are commenting anonymously and sharing your opinion on whether you will ever get married given laws like this and what you think women's actual motivations for marriage are.
